human_reference:
POINTS:
- [hibernate-testing/src/main/java/org/hibernate/testing/orm/junit/DialectFeatureChecks.java:1419] I'm fine with it if you want to skip this for now, but like I wrote, you can emulate this behavior. If `\` is the default escape character, you can implement `s like p escape c` as:

```sql
s like replace(replace(replace(p,c||c,'\\'),c||'%','\%'),c||'?','\?')
```
v8_review:
SUMMARY:
This PR touches 124 files to bring Spanner PostgreSQL dialect test compatibility to life. Core changes include: Docker emulator setup for PostgreSQL dialect, new SpannerPostgreSQLDialect overrides (locking, check constraints, dual table, type handling), scalar subquery emulation for UPDATE statements when FROM clause is unsupported, extensible LIKE escape/pattern hook methods in AbstractSqlAstTranslator, and wide-ranging test annotations plus entity naming fixes to resolve `$` character incompatibilities.

POINTS:
- [hibernate-community-dialects/src/main/java/org/hibernate/community/dialect/SpannerPostgreSQLDialect.java:518-528] Missing `@Override` on `appendDateTimeLiteral(SqlAppender, Calendar, TemporalType, TimeZone)`. The sibling TemporalAccessor and Date overloads both carry `@Override`, and this Calendar overload clearly intends to override `Dialect.appendDateTimeLiteral(Calendar, ...)` (declared at Dialect.java:6068). Add the annotation for consistency and compile-time safety.

- [hibernate-community-dialects/src/main/java/org/hibernate/community/dialect/sql/ast/SpannerPostgreSQLSqlAstTranslator.java:30-38] `renderDmlTargetTableExpression` duplicates the base method body instead of calling `super`. Every other dialect override (PostgreSQLSqlAstTranslator:131, CockroachSqlAstTranslator:114, SpannerSqlAstTranslator:182) calls `super.renderDmlTargetTableExpression(tableReference)` first, then appends custom behavior. Call `super` so this override won't silently miss future base-method enhancements.

- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/sql/ast/spi/AbstractSqlAstTranslator.java:1121-1185] `createScalarSubquery` and `renderSetClauseEmulateScalarSubqueries` follow the established pattern from `determineWhereClauseRestrictionWithJoinEmulation` (same `QuerySpec` + dual-table + join-copy approach). Sound design — just verify the generated SQL is valid when the update has no joins (the subquery would select only the assigned value FROM dual, which is correct).

- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/sql/ast/spi/AbstractSqlAstTranslator.java:8241-8267] New `renderEscapeCharacter` and `renderLikePattern` hooks follow the class's existing extensibility conventions. Note: PostgreSQLSqlAstTranslator overrides `renderLikePredicate` to append `escape ''` when no escape is provided, but does NOT override `renderCaseInsensitiveLikeEmulation` — so case-insensitive LIKE in standard PostgreSQL still lacks that fix (pre-existing gap, not introduced by this PR).

- [hibernate-community-dialects/src/main/java/org/hibernate/community/dialect/SpannerPostgreSQLDialect.java:120-125] `SPANNER_LOCKING_SUPPORT` uses `NoLockingSupport.NO_LOCKING_SUPPORT` with a TODO, yet `getForUpdateString()` still returns `" for update"`. Confirm these methods won't be invoked when locking support reports no-locking, or override them to throw `UnsupportedOperationException` for defensive consistency.

- [docker_db.sh:1568-1631] `spanner_emulator` now installs `gcloud` via curl and configures the emulator instance/database/timezone. The curl-based gcloud install adds a network dependency and could slow CI; consider caching the SDK or documenting this as an explicit CI prerequisite.

- [hibernate-community-dialects/src/main/java/org/hibernate/community/dialect/SpannerPostgreSQLDialect.java:195-280] Three `getCheckCondition` overrides build OR-based check constraints with manual `'` escaping via `value.replace("'", "''")`. This is correct for single-quote escaping, but backslashes and control characters could still cause issues — consider whether a centralized string-escaping utility would be safer here.

- [hibernate-core/src/main/java/org/hibernate/dialect/function/SpannerConcatFunction.java:34-46] `render` produces `concat((arg1)::text,(arg2)::text,...)`. Verify Spanner's CONCAT function actually needs the `::text` casts for all argument types — if inputs are already STRING, the casts are redundant.

- [hibernate-core/src/test/java/org/hibernate/orm/test/annotations/basic/ListOfStringTest.java and ~30 other test files] Widespread `@Entity(name = "...")` additions avoid `$` in entity names (e.g., `OneToManyRefColumnNameTest$That` → `That`). This works but scatters naming workarounds across many test classes. A Spanner-specific naming strategy override might centralize this concern.
